methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 49.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Gravitics is a seed-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Colin Doughan that has raised $20M plus $60M in STRATFI funding. The sector comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace.

Only one valuation method proved applicable to this analysis. Stage-progression anchoring uses the $20M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement in venture capital, yielding $50M. Peer-median analysis could not be applied due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables within Space & Aerospace—while 23 same-sector peers exist, fewer than three met simultaneous filters for stage alignment and valuation tier similarity. Public-comp anchoring was also inapplicable because Gravitics' revenue is not disclosed, precluding use of the sector's 49.8x median P/S multiple.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $42.5M to $57.5M, with a median of $50M. This range reflects high uncertainty inherent in single-method valuation. Key risks include limited peer comparability at the seed stage in frontier aerospace, the treatment of STRATFI capital as raised proceeds without visibility into its deployment constraints, and the absence of revenue-based anchors to validate early-stage assumptions. Additional disclosure of revenue, burn rate, or technical milestones would materially reduce valuation range uncertainty.
